title: Mirror match automations for mulit-connect board

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

At the moment, only mirror columns from single-board-connect-board columns can be used in mirror automations. They should also work with mirror columns from multi-board -connect-board columns.

What are you trying to achieve

Get mirror columns from multi-board-connect-board columns to be available for mirror automations.

posts.author: LamHUan (Huan)
posts.cookedHtml:
That would be a really useful addition. I’ve run into the same limitation — once you move to multi-board connections, a lot of the mirror automation flexibility just disappears.

Being able to use mirror columns from multi-board connect boards would make setups way more scalable, especially for more complex workflows. Definitely feels like a gap worth addressing.

posts.author: Kai_Pioneera (Kai from Pioneera)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a really common pain point. The challenge with mirror columns from multi-board connections is that you’re essentially trying to maintain relationships and sync data across multiple boards, which gets complicated fast.

Have you looked at SIMB (Same Item Multiple Boards)? Instead of using connect boards + mirror columns, you can have the same item exist on multiple boards natively. Any field updates sync across all instances, and you can control which fields sync and which stay board-specific. Works great for cases where you need the same data accessible in multiple contexts without the mirror/connect board complexity.

Worth checking out if you’re hitting these multi-board limitations regularly.

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Mirror columns do have a lot of limitations.

If you’re looking to use regular columns and have the same item on multiple boards you can try the Mirror Item Multiple Boards app.

It allows you to use regular columns and have the same item mirrored on as many boards as you like, without any complex automation setup.
